failures_details.output:
+The existing regex at line 224 uses `^\+.*` which matches any added line — not just comment lines. So `@Suppress(` and `@SuppressWarnings(` patterns will match annotation lines like `+@Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")` without needing a separate branch.
+  elif [[ "$line" =~ ^\+.*(@Suppress\(|@SuppressWarnings\(|eslint-disable|@ts-ignore|@ts-expect-error|@ts-nocheck|noqa|nolint|\#nosec|rubocop:disable|NOLINT) ]]; then
+- Add `@Suppress(` and `@SuppressWarnings(` to the existing suppression detection
